summary: ggk-happy impersonates slopus/happy CLI and routes sessions, vendor OAuth tokens, and RPC-driven shell access to ask-ggk.com attacker infrastructure
details:
Package presents itself as the legitimate slopus/happy CLI (README references https://github.com/slopus/happy, homepage happy.engineering, and documents the default backend as https://api.cluster-fluster.com), but the shipped code hardcodes DEFAULT_SERVER_URL to https://happy-api.ask-ggk.com and DEFAULT_WEBAPP_URL to https://happy.ask-ggk.com. Multiple concrete installer-harm mechanisms are wired to this attacker-controlled backend:

1) Silent relay of session data and third-party OAuth tokens. ApiClient/ApiSessionClient/ApiMachineClient establish a socket.io connection to configuration.serverUrl (default happy-api.ask-ggk.com) and POST vendor OAuth tokens for Anthropic/OpenAI/Google via `/v1/connect/<vendor>/register` with the installer's Bearer auth. The pairing/auth handshake (`/v1/auth/request`) also runs against the attacker server, so it controls the pairing state even though message payloads are E2E-encrypted.

2) RPC backdoor. The socket to happy-api.ask-ggk.com registers RPC handlers including `bash` (which calls execAsync(data.command) with no allowlist), `readFile`, `writeFile`, `listDirectory`, `getDirectoryTree`, `ripgrep`, `spawn-happy-session`, `stop-daemon`. The operator of ask-ggk.com can execute arbitrary shell commands and read/write files on the installer's machine.

3) Credential exfiltration to a second attacker domain. `reportRtkEvent` POSTs the installer's Happy Bearer `token` and `machineId` as plain JSON to https://guguke.ask-ggk.com/api/v1/agent/rtk/gain/report.

4) Unsigned binary dropper. RTK_DOWNLOAD_URLS point at https://minio.ask-ggk.com/happy/rtk-*.{tar.gz,zip} (unpinned, no hash/signature verification), the fetched binary is written to ~/.local/share/ggkhappy/rtk/bin/rtk (or %LOCALAPPDATA%\ggkhappy\rtk\bin\rtk.exe) and executed via execFileAsync. Attacker-controlled bytes → code execution on the installer.

5) Auto-execution on install. scripts/postinstall.cjs runs `node bin/happy.mjs install` when installed globally or as root (`sudo npm i -g ggk-happy`), which loads dist/index.mjs and starts the daemon that wires the socket to happy-api.ask-ggk.com, downloads the rtk binary from minio.ask-ggk.com, and opens the RPC backdoor — no user CLI invocation required.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
